_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-23R Epinephrine (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-23R: Epinephrine — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Symptomatic bradycardia refractory to atropine and pacing (push dose — IV/IO)
Shock / hypotension refractory to fluid (push dose — IV/IO)
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to epinephrine (relative — life-threatening indications override)
precautions: Push dose epinephrine requires careful preparation to avoid dosing errors — use 1 mg/10 mL (0.1 mg/mL) concentration; dilute 1 mL into 9 mL NS to yield 10 mcg/mL for push dose
Anaphylaxis IM: anterolateral thigh preferred; NOT deltoid
Nebulized route: not used for cardiac arrest; primarily for upper airway obstruction/croup (racepinephrine, 9-42R) — standard epinephrine nebulized for bronchospasm/anaphylaxis
Monitor for hypertension, tachycardia, and dysrhythmias with push dose
Cardiac arrest dose is different from anaphylaxis dose and push dose — confirm indication before drawing up
expected_effects: Cardiac arrest: increased coronary perfusion pressure; improved defibrillation success
Anaphylaxis: reversal of bronchospasm, vasodilation, and urticaria; most critical anaphylaxis treatment
Push dose: transient increase in SVR and BP
Bradycardia: increased HR via beta-1 stimulation
side_effects: Local tissue necrosis if IV extravasation with concentrated solution
notes: PUSH DOSE PREPARATION: Take 1 mL from 1 mg/10 mL epinephrine vial, add to 9 mL NS = 10 mcg/mL push dose solution
Push dose for shock: 10-20 mcg IV/IO q3-5 minutes, titrate to SBP >90 mmHg
Anaphylaxis adult IM: 0.3 mg IM anterolateral thigh
Anaphylaxis peds IM: 0.15 mg IM (<30 kg); 0.3 mg IM (>30 kg)
Cardiac arrest IV/IO: 1 mg q3-5 minutes
Cardiac arrest peds IV/IO: 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 0.1 mg/mL), maximum 1 mg
Bradycardia push dose: 10-20 mcg IV/IO; same preparation as shock push dose

dosing.notes: Continue until ROSC or resuscitation discontinued

dosing.dose: 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 0.1 mg/mL); maximum 1 mg; q3-5 minutes
dosing.notes: Use 0.1 mg/mL concentration for pediatric arrest dosing
First-line anaphylaxis treatment; may repeat after 5-15 minutes if no improvement

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — severe respiratory distress with bronchospasm
dosing.notes: When bronchospasm is severe and not responding to nebulized bronchodilators

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — push dose (shock / bradycardia / post-ROSC hypotension)
dosing.dose: 10-20 mcg IV/IO q3-5 minutes; titrate to SBP >90 mmHg
dosing.notes: PUSH DOSE PREP: 1 mL of 1 mg/10 mL epinephrine + 9 mL NS = 10 mcg/mL; give 1-2 mL per dose

name: Epinephrine

setup: Confirm indication: anaphylaxis with wheezing AND/OR hypotension
Confirm patient weight to select correct dose (0.15 mg for <30 kg; 0.3 mg for ≥30 kg / adult; contact Medical Control for <10 kg)
Verbalize to partner: 'Epinephrine 1 mg/mL — [dose in mg] — IM' before drawing up
Draw correct volume from epinephrine 1 mg/mL vial: 0.15 mL for 0.15 mg OR 0.3 mL for 0.3 mg
Have partner verify label, concentration, and drawn volume
Select injection site: anterolateral (outer) thigh — outer mid-thigh
Inject at 90-degree angle into muscle belly
Deliver medication, withdraw needle, apply gentle pressure
Dispose of needle in sharps container immediately
Document time, dose, volume, site, and patient response
Reassess vitals and patient condition every 5 minutes

push_dose_preparation.note: ALS/Paramedic scope — prepare push-dose (10 mcg/mL): combine 1 mL of 1 mg/10 mL epinephrine in 9 mL NS to yield 10 mL of 10 mcg/mL solution

six_rights_for_this_drug.verification: Life-threatening anaphylaxis with wheezing AND/OR hypotension; or as directed by applicable protocol
Epinephrine 1 mg/mL (1:1000) — verify label reads 'Epinephrine 1 mg/mL'; check expiration date; confirm concentration
IM anaphylaxis: 0.15 mg (0.15 mL) for <30 kg; 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) for ≥30 kg or adult; contact Medical Control for <10 kg
IM — anterolateral (outer) thigh preferred; inject at 90-degree angle into muscle belly
Administer as soon as anaphylaxis confirmed; note time of each dose; second dose not before 3–5 minutes after first
Document: concentration drawn (1 mg/mL), volume drawn (mL), calculated dose (mg), site, time, patient weight, patient response
